title: Why I Stopped Writing Microservices
description: A critical look at when microservices hurt more than they help — and the architectural patterns that replaced them in our stack.

title: Building a 50k TPS Fraud Detection System in Go
description: A deep dive into stream processing architecture, Redis data structures, and the tradeoffs we made to hit our latency targets without sacrificing accuracy.

title: The Hidden Costs of Distributed Transactions
description: Two-phase commit, sagas, and outbox patterns explained through real incidents. When correctness comes at the price of availability.

title: How We Cut P99 Latency by 40% Without Rewriting Anything
description: Profiling, connection pool tuning, and query plan analysis — a systematic approach to performance engineering that doesn't require a ground-up rewrite.

title: Designing APIs That Last
description: Versioning strategies, backwards compatibility, and the API design principles we've learned from maintaining public APIs used by millions of developers.
